title: Centaurium caspicum
taxonomy: Centaurium caspicum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the Caspian region and is a perennial herb. The plant is often found in mountainous areas and is known for its blue or purple flowers. Centaurium caspicum is closely related to other species within the Centaurium genus, which is a group of plants commonly known as "centaury". The exact classification of Centaurium caspicum is still a topic of ongoing research and study.
uses: Centaurium caspicum, a species of plant in the gentian family, has been found to have various medicinal properties. Its extracts have been traditionally used to treat kidney and urinary issues, as well as to reduce inflammation. In some cultures, Centaurium caspicum is used to treat digestive problems and alleviate symptoms of fever. Additionally, its antioxidant properties are said to contribute to the plant's potential anti-aging and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and benefits.

constituents: Centaurium caspicum, also known as the Caspian centaury or Russian centaury, is a plant native to the Caucasus and Iran. Its active constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant's flavonoids, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
